When the siege had begun, Zedekiah sent to ask Jeremiah about what would happen. People often seek those they despise and oppose—those who can counsel and pray for them—especially in times of distress and danger; but they only want deliverance from punishment. If people continue in disobedience, presuming upon outward privileges, let them know that the Lord will prosper his open enemies against them. Because the king and his princes would not surrender, the people are urged to do so. No sinner on earth is left without a Refuge who really desires one; but the way of life is humbling, it requires self-denial, and exposes to difficulties.

The wickedness of the king and his family was made worse by their relation to David. They were urged to act with justice immediately, lest the Lord's anger should be unquenchable. If God is for us, who can be against us? But if he is against us, who can do anything for us?
